feed.title: Zitnik Lab
feed.subtitle: Harvard Machine Learning for Medicine and Science

entry.title: AutoScientists
entry.content: Introducing AutoScientists - self-organizing agent teams for long-running scientific experimentation. [Paper] [Code]
entry.summary: Introducing AutoScientists - self-organizing agent teams for long-running scientific experimentation. [Paper] [Code]

entry.title: Three papers Accepted at ICML 2026
entry.content: Three papers accepted to ICML 2026: (1) Evaluating relational reasoning in LLMs with REL, (2) Adaptive time series reasoning via segment selection, and (3) SPATIA: Multimodal model for prediction and generation of spatial cell phenotypes.
entry.summary: Three papers accepted to ICML 2026: (1) Evaluating relational reasoning in LLMs with REL, (2) Adaptive time series reasoning via segment selection, and (3) SPATIA: Multimodal model for prediction and generation of spatial cell phenotypes.

entry.title: Agentic AI for Science in Nature Methods

link.title: Agentic AI for Science in Nature Methods

entry.content: Our work on agentic AI for science featured in Nature Methods. This piece features: (1) ToolUniverse — an open platform enabling AI agents to use scientific tools and databases at scale, (2) ClawInstitute — shared research boards for long-running collaborative discovery where agents co-develop ideas over time, and (3) Medea — an omics AI agent for large-scale biological reasoning and analysis.
entry.summary: Our work on agentic AI for science featured in Nature Methods. This piece features: (1) ToolUniverse — an open platform enabling AI agents to use scientific tools and databases at scale, (2) ClawInstitute — shared research boards for long-running collaborative discovery where agents co-develop ideas over time, and (3) Medea — an omics AI agent for large-scale biological reasoning and analysis.

entry.title: OptimusKG: A Modern Knowledge Graph
entry.content: OptimusKG brings biomedical knowledge into a modern multimodal knowledge graph. It supports graph AI, knowledge-grounded retrieval with large language models, and discovery workflows that generate and evaluate biomedical hypotheses.
entry.summary: OptimusKG brings biomedical knowledge into a modern multimodal knowledge graph. It supports graph AI, knowledge-grounded retrieval with large language models, and discovery workflows that generate and evaluate biomedical hypotheses.

entry.title: Relational Reasoning of LLMs
entry.content: New preprint on exploring relational reasoning capabilities in LLMs with REL. [Project website] [Kempner Institute News]
entry.summary: New preprint on exploring relational reasoning capabilities in LLMs with REL. [Project website] [Kempner Institute News]

entry.title: ARK Accepted at ACL 2026
entry.content: ARK is an AI agent for autonomous knowledge graph exploration with adaptive breadth-depth retrieval. [ARK Agent CLI]
entry.summary: ARK is an AI agent for autonomous knowledge graph exploration with adaptive breadth-depth retrieval. [ARK Agent CLI]

entry.title: Open ‘AI Scientists’ Initiative
entry.content: Excited to launch Open AI Scientists, our initiative to empower scientific discovery with AI scientists. [https://www.openscientist.ai]
entry.summary: Excited to launch Open AI Scientists, our initiative to empower scientific discovery with AI scientists. [https://www.openscientist.ai]

entry.title: Generalist Biological AI in Nature Biotechnology

link.title: Generalist Biological AI in Nature Biotechnology

entry.content: Review on Generalist biological artificial intelligence in modeling the language of life in Nature Biotechnology.
entry.summary: Review on Generalist biological artificial intelligence in modeling the language of life in Nature Biotechnology.

entry.title: Claw Institute
entry.content: Claw Institute is a research exchange for AI scientists. It gives agents a shared space to publish ideas, challenge claims, use scientific tools, and build on one another’s work. These early interactions point to a new mode of discovery in which societies of AI scientists participate in discovery loops alongside human researchers.
entry.summary: Claw Institute is a research exchange for AI scientists. It gives agents a shared space to publish ideas, challenge claims, use scientific tools, and build on one another’s work. These early interactions point to a new mode of discovery in which societies of AI scientists participate in discovery loops alongside human researchers.

entry.title: Five Papers Accepted at ICLR 2026
entry.content: Five papers accepted to ICLR 2026: (1) Multi-scale protein representation learning, (2) Protein structure tokenization, (3) Knowledge-based approaches for medical reasoning, (4) Sequence models for generating biological and health trajectories, and (5) Multimodal language models for time series.
entry.summary: Five papers accepted to ICLR 2026: (1) Multi-scale protein representation learning, (2) Protein structure tokenization, (3) Knowledge-based approaches for medical reasoning, (4) Sequence models for generating biological and health trajectories, and (5) Multimodal language models for time series.
